questions.question: What is the primary function of a flip-flop in digital circuits?
options: To store a single bit of binary information (state).
To perform arithmetic and logical operations.
To convert analog signals to digital signals.
To select one of multiple input signals.
To generate a clock signal.

questions.question: In the physical design stage of an Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC), what is the primary objective of the "Place and Route" process?
options: To convert the Register Transfer Level (RTL) code into a gate-level netlist.
To verify the logical correctness of the design using simulation.
To determine the optimal physical locations for standard cells and then connect them with wires.
To synthesize the clock distribution network for minimal skew.
To generate test vectors for manufacturing defect detection.

questions.question: In an out-of-order execution processor, what is the primary function of the Reorder Buffer (ROB)?
options: To store micro-operations (μops) that are ready for execution.
To predict the outcome of branch instructions to avoid pipeline stalls.
To buffer instructions that have completed execution but are waiting to update architectural state in program order.
To temporarily hold data fetched from main memory before it's written to cache.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of physical registers for register renaming.

questions.question: What is the primary characteristic of "memory-mapped I/O" in computer architecture?
options: I/O devices communicate with the CPU exclusively through dedicated I/O instructions.
I/O devices are assigned unique memory addresses, allowing the CPU to access them using regular load/store instructions.
A separate bus is used for all I/O transactions, independent of the main memory bus.
I/O operations are always handled by a Direct Memory Access (DMA) controller without CPU involvement.
It requires a dedicated I/O controller with its own instruction set.

questions.question: Compared to a fully synchronous digital design, what is the primary advantage of employing asynchronous (clockless) design principles for certain parts of a complex integrated circuit?
options: Simplified timing verification due to the absence of clock-related issues.
Guaranteed higher maximum operating frequency for the entire chip.
Potentially lower power consumption and better resilience to process variations and voltage fluctuations.
Elimination of all power integrity issues across the design.
Significantly reduced design complexity and faster time-to-market.
